title: Sweet orange reed diffuser
subtitle: A citrus reed diffuser with jojoba oil and sweet orange essential oil
excerpt: A reed diffuser made from jojoba oil and sweet orange essential oil at 25% of the oil weight. Jojoba oil is lighter than most fixed oils and wicks up the diffuser reeds without the heavy residue that thick carrier oils can leave. A small amount of lemon essential oil sharpens the top note. Fill into a 100 ml amber glass bottle and insert 6 to 8 rattan reeds.
sourceNotes: Bramble Berry reed diffuser base guidance (brambleberry.com, accessed May 2026): jojoba as a carrier for natural reed diffusers. Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014): sweet orange and lemon EO non-phototoxicity in diffuser context.

recipe.batchNotes: Fill multiple bottles at once. The blend keeps for 6 months sealed.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Fill and seal, then insert the reeds when you want the fragrance to begin. Once the reeds are inserted, the diffusion starts immediately.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Once the reeds are inserted, the diffuser lasts 4 to 8 weeks depending on room temperature and ventilation. A warmer room or a position near an air vent shortens the duration. The sealed oil blend keeps for 6 months before opening.

glossaryTerms.definition: Rattan reeds absorb the oil blend by capillary action and allow it to evaporate slowly from the exposed top surface. The rate of evaporation depends on the oil's viscosity (thinner oils travel up the reed faster), room temperature (warmer rooms increase evaporation), and airflow. Flipping the reeds once a week reverses the saturated and dry ends, temporarily increasing the scent throw.

glossaryTerms.term: Essential oil load in a reed diffuser
glossaryTerms.definition: Reed diffusers typically use 20 to 30% essential oil or fragrance oil by weight in the carrier oil. A higher load produces a stronger throw; a lower load lasts longer. At 25%, this diffuser gives a moderate-strength citrus scent. Because the oil is not heated or combusted, there is no flash-point concern: the same safety constraints as candles do not apply.

content.text: A reed diffuser releases fragrance passively through
and evaporation, with no heat or flame. Jojoba oil is an effective natural carrier because it is lighter in viscosity than most fixed oils and travels up the reed readily. The
of 25% gives a moderate citrus scent throw. Lemon essential oil adds a sharper, fresher top note to the rounder sweet orange base.
Do not place the diffuser on polished furniture; the oil can mark or stain the surface if spilled. Keep the bottle on a ceramic tile or a small dish. Keep out of reach of children and away from pets.

items.prepNote: Golden or clear jojoba oil. A lighter, more fluid carrier than coconut or sweet almond oil for reed diffuser use.
20% of total blend weight. Cold-pressed sweet orange peel oil. The primary citrus note.
5% of total blend weight. Adds brightness and a sharper top note above the sweet orange.

content.text: Weigh the jojoba oil, sweet orange essential oil, and lemon essential oil into a small jug. Stir briefly to combine. Using the funnel, pour into the amber glass bottle. Seal and label with the making date.

When ready to begin diffusing, open the bottle and insert 6 to 8 rattan diffuser reeds. The reeds will begin wicking oil within a few hours; the full scent throw develops over the first 24 hours. To boost the scent, flip the reeds once a week so the oil-saturated ends are exposed to the air.

Once the reeds are inserted, the diffuser typically lasts 4 to 8 weeks. A sealed, unfilled bottle of the oil blend keeps for 6 months away from heat and light. The citrus top notes fade faster than the base note; after 4 to 6 weeks in use, the sweet orange becomes more prominent as the lemon evaporates away. Refill with a fresh batch when the bottle runs out.
